seoTitle: Lansdowne vs Laramba — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lansdowne and Laramba. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lansdowne and Laramba.
heading: Lansdowne vs Laramba — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lansdowne, Northern Territory and Laramba, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Laramba has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Lansdowne (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lansdowne or Laramba safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lansdowne has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Laramba scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lansdowne scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Laramba scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lansdowne or Laramba?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Laramba has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
